name: Johnny Otis
abstract: Johnny Otis (December 28, 1921 – January 17, 2012), born Ioannis Alexandres Veliotes, was an American singer, musician, composer, bandleader, record producer, talent scout, and preacher. He was a seminal influence on American R&B and rock and roll.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: The Johnny Otis Show

about_quotes.speaker: Plas Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I met and played with Johnny Otis. Johnny Otis had his going Rhythm & Blues Revue going at the time, and I played with him a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Later on, I left California with Johnny Otis. ... I was back east with Johnny Otis’ band. ... He asked me would I go with the band, and I said yes, a thousand times.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Richard Wyands
about_quotes.evidence: I took lessons, actually I took lessons from Johnny Otis, who lived in the neighborhood.
about_quotes.doc_title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: have played some dates with Johnny Otis's band... We worked with Johnny Otis

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: What happened is Johnny Otis was at the Club Alabam, which was 3 doors down... Stan Kenton came on the Avenue because he was looking for a drummer, and someone told him to come hear Johnny Otis.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions working in Johnny Otis' band, but the interviewee (Ernie Fields) does not claim any personal connection to Johnny Otis in his response.
